item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A really interesting idea that I enjoyed reading, but I have a slight issue with the end. It felt like you were building to something big, only for the end to lack any kind of "punch". Apart from that, neat concept yo. +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A wildly interesting idea with a sub-par excecution. There are tonal and phrasal problems all throughout the article.

The structuring of messages received from SCP-3658-1 don't make sense - why is the time and location of the next message sent at the end of each message, after the portions which cannot be decoded? Sure, you can decode it backwards from the end, but that seems needlessly complicated when you could just state this information at the beginning of the message. Seems also pointless to always have a big wall of 'DO NOT READ' at the midsection of every message.

The origins of SCP-3658 sound like the blandest cup of sci-fi-garbage that I've ever seen posted on this site. Furthermore, why would the Foundation threated SCP-3658-1 by 'excecution' though the Large Hadron Collider, when this obviously wouldn't kill SCP-3658 itself, rather than its only relay with the main universe? This would be counterproductive for both parties. Why even use the LHC, a massively public institution that's bound to be teeming with civilian scientists and everything is recorded with scrutiny? Wouldn't a normal particle collider work?

This article doesn't do justice to its concept.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Currently, you have two unsourced images within your article. Please source these images by replying to the sent PM and/or this post within 48 hours or they will have to be deleted. Also, make sure the images comply with the SCP Wiki's CC-BY-SA 3.0 license by following this handy guide, otherwise they will have to be deleted.

48 hours have elapsed, and thus, the image(s) have been removed. Please PM with the source if you wish to have them restored or if you have any questions. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really good research and detail here, but tonally it just… doesn't feel like the SCP Foundation to me. Too much sci-fi, not enough cryptid. On the other hand, it is a well thought-out concept with a lot of clear effort put into it. Neutral vote here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was ready to fall off the wagon when the subatomic particle turned out to be a sentient being capable of communicating in English. But I feel you justified it well with the time travel paradox and the interviews are fun. However, I also found the ending to be lacking and unsatisfactory. It ends right when it’s getting really interesting. If you trimmed and consolidated what came before to give more room to expand on your conclusion this would be much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
well written with an interesting idea.

+1 from me
